'''Jason Temu''' (born 17 April 1972) former [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]], and club level for the [[Marist Saints]], [[Counties Manukau rugby league team#1994-1996: Heroes|Counties Manukau]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham Bears]], [[Hull FC]], [[Newcastle Knights]] and the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

==Background==
Temu was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] and is of [[Cook Islanders]] descent.

==Early years==
Temu grew up in New Zealand and played for the [[Marist Saints]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]].<ref name=CM>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.106</ref> In 1994 he followed his coach, [[Stan Martin (rugby league)|Stan Martin]], to the [[Counties Manukau rugby league team#1994-1996: Heroes|Counties Manukau Heroes]] in the new [[Lion Red Cup]] competition.<ref name=LRC>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.94</ref> After several strong seasons with the Heroes he was offered a professional contract to play for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]], and then for the renamed [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham Bears]] in the [[Super League]]. At Oldham Bears, Temu made an immediate impression, playing the first 22 consecutive games.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-curtain-to-fall-at-troubled-doncaster-1612451.html |title=RUGBY LEAGUE: Curtain to fall at troubled Doncaster |author=Dave Hadfield |work=The Independent |date=23 March 1995 |access-date=8 September 2020}}</ref> Temu was to play for the club for the next three years.<ref>[http://rugby-league-world.com/1997/Results/StonesSL/Oldham.html Oldham stats] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081204145525/http://www.rugby-league-world.com/1997/Results/StonesSL/Oldham.html |date=4 December 2008 }} ''rugby-league-world.com''</ref>

In 1995, after his first season with Oldham Bears, Temu was selected for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Island]] to play in the [[1995 Rugby League Emerging Nations Tournament|Emerging Nations Tournament]].<ref>[http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm 1995 World Cup] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070929104732/http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm |date=2007-09-29}} ''The Vault''</ref> The Cook Islands won the tournament.

==Hull Sharks and Newcastle Knights==
In 1998 Temu made the move to the [[Hull F.C.|Hull Sharks]] and the [[Super League]]. Temu played 15 games for the club that year.

In 1999 Temu moved to Australia and joined the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]] competition. His time at the club was plagued by injury and over two seasons he only played 3 first grade games. He was released at the end of 2000.<ref>[http://dazrl.awardspace.info/2000_mov.htm 2000 Player Movements] ''Darryl's Rugby League''</ref>

==World Cup and Warriors==

At the end of 2000 Temu was selected in the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Island]] team for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] and was made vice-captain of the squad.

The [[Wigan Warriors]] were interested in signing Temu, who did not count as a quota player due to the time he had spent in Great Britain.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/leeds-pair-opt-for-way-of-the-welsh-dragon-700446.html Leeds pair opt for way of the Welsh dragon] ''The Independent'', 26 September 2000</ref> However he was denied a work permit due to the little game time he had had at Newcastle and so the deal fell through.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/bradford-reward-nobles-loyalty-636038.html Bradford reward Noble's loyalty] ''The Independent'', 6 October 2000</ref>

Instead, Temu signed with the new [[New Zealand Warriors]] club and provided experience to the younger props in the side. When not selected by the first grade side he played for the [[Marist Richmond Brothers]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]].<ref>[https://archive.today/20120913024814/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=6157 Round 7 Bartercard Cup Teams] ''Rleague.com'', 26 April 2001</ref> He played four first grade games for the Warriors and subsequently decided to retire at the end of the 2001 season.
